video:video.video:title: Women are dying in Africa as US ramps up its global battle against abortion
video:video.video:description: Women are dying in Africa as US ramps up its global battle against abortion

video:video.video:title: Puerto Ricans react to announcement of emergency water rationing as drought worsens
video:video.video:description: Water rationing measures were set to take effect in several communities in Puerto Rico on Friday as a drought grips the U.S. territory.

video:video.video:title: Sirens and explosions in Kuwait City as Iran and US escalate strikes
video:video.video:description: Kuwait announced on Saturday that its air defense systems had intercepted and downed Iranian missiles launched at the tiny Gulf State.

video:video.video:title: Brad Paisley on Taylor Swift’s wedding
video:video.video:description: Brad Paisley says Taylor Swift’s wedding was one of the least pretentious he has attended or will ever see.

video:video.video:title: D-Day landing beaches bid for UNESCO World Heritage status
video:video.video:description: The beaches where Allied forces landed for the D-Day operation could be awarded UNESCO World Heritage Status.

video:video.video:title: New footage of Monaco bombing released by Ukraine's general prosecutor
video:video.video:description: Ukrainian prosecutors have released what they say is video of the explosion in Monaco that seriously injured tycoon Vadym Yermolaiev and his family.

video:video.video:title: World Cup beer sales are hopping. Brewers hope the stout demand outlasts the soccer tournament
video:video.video:description: The World Cup has been a bonanza for beer. Bars in Boston needed emergency deliveries after some taps ran dry. (AP Video by Rodrique Ngowi)

video:video.video:title: Massive fire becomes Norway's largest in modern times after destroying more than 100 homes
video:video.video:description: The largest residential fire in modern Norwegian history destroyed more than 100 homes and forced hundreds of people to evacuate, authorities said Saturday.

video:video.video:title: Inside Tasmania's newest library, home to a copy of Shakespeare's First Folio
video:video.video:description: As this year's Dark Mofo art festival came to an end, the Museum of Old and New Art (MONA) in Hobart unveiled its largest expansion since opening 15 years ago.

video:video.video:title: Local Chinese residents join the landslide relief effort in Chongqing
video:video.video:description: The landslide occurred in Pengshui County on Friday morning on the outskirts of Chongqing municipality, when massive amounts of rocks and soil washed down a slope, burying more than 10 residential buildings, state broadcaster CCTV said.

video:video.video:title: How the Strait of Hormuz is monitored as US-Iran conflict escalates
video:video.video:description: The United States intensified its strikes on Iran early Thursday, hitting targets further north as American forces also fired into a ship the U.S. accused of trying to break its naval blockade on the Islamic Republic.

video:video.video:title: Thousands attend funeral for Pakistani police officers killed by insurgents in Quetta
video:video.video:description: Thousands of people attended funeral prayers in Quetta on Saturday for Pakistani police officers killed by insurgents last week.

video:video.video:title: Trial of roadside virtual fence tech to reduce vehicle collisions with wildlife
video:video.video:description: The local government in the Australian city of Canberra once again faced calls to explore a trial of roadside "virtual fence" technology to reduce vehicle collisions with wildlife.

video:video.video:title: River Danube in Romania reaches record low flow in 30 years
video:video.video:description: The Danube River fell to its lowest flow in 30 years on Thursday and continued to drop through Saturday, with the downward trend expected to hold at least until Monday, Romania's National Waters Administration said.

video:video.video:title: AP top stories July 18
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Saturday, July 18th: US military says 2 troops are dead and 1 missing after Iran attacks; Ukrainian drones hit Russian warehouses; Massive fire becomes Norway's largest in modern times; Argentina and Spanish fans ready for final.

video:video.video:title: As war roils in Iran, health workers in Nigeria says more children are relapsing into malnutrition
video:video.video:description: Health workers in northern Nigeria say more children are relapsing into malnutrition and cite knock-on effects from the war in the Middle East.

video:video.video:title: Women amputees in South America train to be World Cup soccer contenders
video:video.video:description: Women amputees from several countries are training in Bolivia for the 2027 Women's Amputee Football World Cup.

video:video.video:title: Buildings damaged in Kyiv after authorities report an overnight Russian attack
video:video.video:description: One person was killed and eight others injured in an overnight Russian attack on Kyiv that included ballistic missiles, local authorities said.

video:video.video:title: Firefighters struggle to control huge Zaragoza wildfire in Spain
video:video.video:description: The fire in "Cinco Villas", in the province of Zaragoza, remained out of control on Saturday, as shown by images captured by drones from the Civil Guard’s Pegaso team.

video:video.video:title: Bridge near Bandar Abbas severely damaged in latest US strikes
video:video.video:description: Overnight U.S. strikes damaged a bridge and two tunnels, disrupting one of the main highways towards Bandar Abbas, a city which sits near the narrowest part of the Strait of Hormuz, according to Iran's state-run news agency.

video:video.video:title: US military ends latest airstrikes on Iran
video:video.video:description: The U.S. military launched airstrikes Sunday targeting Iran's paramilitary Revolutionary Guard to retaliate for the killing of American troops in Jordan, further widening the crossfire between the nations as they fight over Strait of Hormuz.

video:video.video:title: Argentina fans unfurl giant a Messi jersey in his hometown ahead of World Cup final
video:video.video:description: Fans unfurled a giant Lionel Messi jersey in the Argentine city of Rosario on Saturday, thanking the soccer star ahead of the World Cup final.

video:video.video:title: Andrew Tate and his brother Tristan arrested in Miami, US Marshals service tells AP
video:video.video:description: Influencer brothers Andrew and Tristan Tate have been arrested by federal authorities in Miami. A spokesperson for the U.S.

video:video.video:title: Argentina and Spain fans descend on New York for World Cup final
video:video.video:description: Jubilant Spain and Argentina fans were full of joy Saturday, dancing and singing in New York ahead of the World Cup final on Sunday.

video:video.video:title: South Korea awaits UNESCO decision on biodiversity haven Garorim Bay
video:video.video:description: South Korea's tidal flats are renowned for their wealth of biodiversity, some are inscribed on UNESCO's World Heritage List, but one has been overlooked until now.

video:video.video:title: 1 killed, 8 injured in an overnight Russian attack on Kyiv
video:video.video:description: One person was killed and eight others injured in an overnight Russian attack on Kyiv that included ballistic missiles, local authorities said.

video:video.video:title: The AI voice app that detects COPD flare-ups earlier
video:video.video:description: AI could help COPD patients check for flare-ups from home. It analyzes a patient's voice to determine if the incurable lung condition is about to become worse.

video:video.video:title: Rescuers search rubble after 5.5 magnitude earthquake hits Peru's Andes region, killing at least 5
video:video.video:description: Rescuers search rubble after 5.5 magnitude earthquake hits Peru's Andes region, killing at least 5

video:video.video:title: Italy's historic 'people's theatres' hope for UNESCO World Heritage status
video:video.video:description: Italy's stunning community theatres are hoping for UNESCO World Heritage recognition this weekend. The cultural centres were built during the 18th and 19th centuries and still entertain local people to this day.

video:video.video:title: Video circulating appears to show moment Iran missiles hit Jordan base, killing 2 US service members
video:video.video:description: Video footage circulating online appears to show the moment Iranian missiles hit an airbase in Jordan, killing two American service members.

video:video.video:title: Video circulating appears to show moment Iran missiles hit Jordan base, killing 2 US service members
video:video.video:description: Video footage circulating online appears to show the moment Iranian missiles hit an airbase in Jordan, killing two American service members.

video:video.video:title: Soccer fans flock to 2026 World Cup final
video:video.video:description: Thousands of fans lined up hours before the 2026 World Cup final hoping to watch their team hoist the FIFA World Cup Trophy. (AP Video: Emily Wang)

video:video.video:title: Trump flies over stadium in Marine One before attending the World Cup final
video:video.video:description: The U.S. presidential helicopter, Marine One, flew over the stadium hosting the World Cup final, heralding the arrival of President Donald Trump. (AP video: Emily Wang)

video:video.video:title: Cuban artist and dissident exiled from country after 5 years in prison arrives in US
video:video.video:description: Famous Cuban dissident artist, Luis Manuel Otero Alcántara, arrived in Miami on Saturday after being released from a five-year prison sentence on the condition that he leave his country.

video:video.video:title: AP top stories July 19
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Sunday, July 19: US launches more airstrikes at Iran; a 5.5 magnitude quake shakes Peru; Recue crews work to find landslide survivors in China; FIFA World Cup final kicks off in New Jersey

video:video.video:title: What fans will remember most about the World Cup
video:video.video:description: From unforgettable matches to breakout stars and shared watch parties, fans reflected on the moments that made this World Cup one to remember.

video:video.video:title: Rescuers find 5 survivors and are still looking for 20 others after boat sinks in Indonesia
video:video.video:description: Rescuers on Saturday evening found five survivors, including a 7-year-old girl, from a passenger boat that sank earlier in the week in eastern Indonesia waters and were searching for 20 other people still missing.

video:video.video:title: Why Britain is getting a new prime minister without a general election
video:video.video:description: Veteran Labour Party politician and popular former Greater Manchester mayor Andy Burnham is set to become Britain’s next prime minister on Monday, formally taking over from Keir Starmer, who announced his resignation last month.

video:video.video:title: Fans react outside the stadium after Spain win the World Cup by beating Argentina 1-0
video:video.video:description: Soccer fans reacted on Sunday after watching Spain beat Argentina to win the World Cup for a second time in its history. (AP video shot by Emily Wang)

video:video.video:title: A rare double tornado seen in Sweden
video:video.video:description: A rare double tornado was caught on camera swirling over water in Sweden on Saturday. The two funnels later merged into one as the storm passed over Grästorp.

video:video.video:title: CENTCOM video released as US launches more airstrikes against Iran
video:video.video:description: The latest attacks again showed how, step by step, the U.S. and Iran have inched closer to all-out war as last month’s interim deal meant to permanently end the fighting has crumbled and shipping traffic in the Strait of Hormuz has largely stalled.
